title: Validate every IPC message in the main process
description: Treat all IPC payloads from renderers as untrusted input; validate sender, channel, and arguments before acting.
body: Use when handling ipcMain.handle/on messages. Do not trust the channel name, argument types, or file paths a renderer sends, and do not perform privileged actions (file write, shell, network) on raw input. Actions: validate the payload shape/types, check the sender frame where relevant, constrain file paths to allowed directories, and reject anything unexpected. Validation signals include handlers that reject malformed or out-of-bounds requests in tests. Risk boundary: a path-traversal or command argument injected through IPC turns a renderer compromise into arbitrary file or command access via the privileged main process — main-process input validation is mandatory, the renderer is not a trust boundary.
